navigation_overview:
The top navigation bar appears on every page and contains the site name "MoodleTest" on the left, which links to the home page, followed by links for Home, Dashboard, and My Courses. On the right side, a notifications bell icon shows alerts when clicked, a messaging icon opens the messaging drawer, and the user's initials in a circular icon opens the user menu dropdown. The user menu contains links for Profile, Grades, Calendar, Private Files, Reports, and Preferences, followed by a Log out option that ends the session and returns to the login page. When viewing a course, a horizontal tab bar appears below the course title with tabs for Course, Participants, Grades, Activities, and Competencies. Students do not see the Settings tab. When viewing an activity, breadcrumbs at the top show the full navigation path from the course, with each segment as a clickable link. The Course Index sidebar on the left side of course pages provides a hierarchical tree of all sections and activities, with expand/collapse arrows per section and a close button (X) to hide the sidebar. The currently active item is highlighted.

---

module_summaries.summary:
Line 1: Users can authenticate by entering username and password, access the site as a guest, or view cookie information.
Line 2: Shows Username and Password fields, Log in button, inline error messages on failure, and links/buttons for Lost password, Access as a guest, and Cookies notice.

Line 1: Users can view and filter upcoming activities, change timeline range/sort/search, navigate the calendar, and create new events.
Line 2: Displays a personalized greeting, a Timeline of upcoming activities/deadlines, and a monthly Calendar view with current month/year and course filter.

Line 1: Users can browse their enrolled courses, change view layout, filter/search/sort courses, star (pin) courses, and remove courses from view.
Line 2: Displays course cards with banner image, clickable course name, category, and controls for status filter, search, sort, and layout.

Line 1: Users can browse course content, expand or collapse sections, and open activities and resources to navigate to them.
Line 2: Displays the course title, navigation tabs, a list of collapsible sections and their activities/resources with type icons and clickable names.

Line 1: Users can filter and build complex conditions to find participants, apply/clear filters, and use alphabetical filters to narrow results.
Line 2: Displays a participants table with checkboxes, sortable linked First/Last names, Roles, Groups, and Last access to course.

Line 1: Users can view their course grade report and expand/collapse grade item groups to inspect individual graded activities.
Line 2: Displays grade items with calculated weights, earned grade or placeholder, range, percentage, feedback, contribution, and a course total aggregation row.

Line 1: Users can view assignment details and submit work via an Add submission form supporting online text and/or file uploads.
Line 2: Displays Opened and Due dates, full Description, and a Submission status summary table showing Submission status, Grading status, Time remaining, Last modified, and related submission fields.

Line 1: Users can browse a consolidated list of all course activities grouped by type, expand/collapse groups, and navigate to any activity.
Line 2: Displays grouped sections (Assignments, Forums, Resources, etc.) with tables showing activity Name (clickable), parent section, Due date, and Submission status where applicable.

Line 1: Users can view a student's profile, send a message, and navigate to edit profile or related reports and course links.
Line 2: Displays the user's initials icon, full name, optional description, User details (email, visibility, timezone, Edit profile link), privacy/policy links, enrolled course links, and report links.

Line 1: Users can terminate their authenticated session by selecting Log out.
Line 2: Performs logout and redirects to the Login page, preventing access to protected pages until re-authentication.

test_cases.title: Login with valid credentials
test_cases.preconditions: None
steps: Fill all required fields (Username, Password) with valid credentials
Click "Log in"
test_cases.expected_result: Valid credentials redirect the student to the Dashboard.

test_cases.title: Access as a guest offers unauthenticated browsing
test_cases.preconditions: None
steps: Click "Access as a guest"
test_cases.expected_result: Access as a guest offers unauthenticated browsing.

test_cases.title: Submit with all required fields empty
test_cases.preconditions: None
steps: Leave all required fields empty
Click "Log in"
test_cases.expected_result: Inline error message is shown; password field is cleared; username retained for correction.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Invalid or empty credentials show an inline error message

test_cases.title: Submit with Username field empty
test_cases.preconditions: None
steps: Fill all other required fields, leave Username empty
Click "Log in"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation error indicating Username is required; password field is cleared.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Username is required

test_cases.title: Submit with Password field empty
test_cases.preconditions: None
steps: Fill all other required fields, leave Password empty
Click "Log in"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation error indicating Password is required; password field is cleared; username retained for correction.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Password is required

test_cases.title: Login with invalid credentials
test_cases.preconditions: None
steps: Fill all required fields (Username with existing account identifier, Password with incorrect value)
Click "Log in"
test_cases.expected_result: Inline error message is shown; password field is cleared; username retained for correction.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Invalid or empty credentials clear the password field

test_cases.title: Lost password? link is disabled on test site
test_cases.preconditions: None
steps: Verify the "Lost password?" link is present and attempt to activate it (click it to confirm no navigation)
test_cases.expected_result: The "Lost password?" link is disabled on the test site and does not navigate.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Lost password? link is disabled on this test site

test_cases.title: Cookies notice displays cookie usage information
test_cases.preconditions: None
steps: Verify Cookies notice is visible and contains cookie usage information
If a dismiss control is present, optionally dismiss the notice
test_cases.expected_result: Cookies notice provides cookie usage information.

test_cases.title: Update Timeline when a time range is selected
test_cases.workflow: Select time range using time range dropdown to update Timeline
test_cases.preconditions: Timeline block is visible on the Dashboard and the user is enrolled in at least one course
steps: Select a time range from the time range dropdown
Observe the Timeline block update
test_cases.expected_result: Timeline displays activities and deadlines that fall within the selected time range.

test_cases.title: Timeline aggregates activities across all enrolled courses for selected time range
test_cases.workflow: Select time range using time range dropdown to update Timeline
test_cases.preconditions: User is enrolled in multiple courses and at least one enrolled course has upcoming activities within the selected time range
steps: Select a time range from the time range dropdown
Verify the Timeline lists activities from each enrolled course that have items in the selected range
test_cases.expected_result: Timeline shows upcoming activities and deadlines from all enrolled courses that fall within the selected time range.

test_cases.title: Timeline updates when switching between different time ranges
test_cases.workflow: Select time range using time range dropdown to update Timeline
test_cases.preconditions: Timeline block is visible and there are activities present in at least one selectable time range
steps: Select a first time range from the time range dropdown and note the displayed Timeline items
Select a different time range from the time range dropdown
Observe the Timeline block update to reflect the newly selected range
test_cases.expected_result: Timeline updates to show activities corresponding to the newly selected time range (or shows the empty state if none exist).

test_cases.title: Change sort order updates Timeline order
test_cases.workflow: Change sort order using sort order dropdown to update Timeline
test_cases.preconditions: User is on Dashboard with Timeline block visible and multiple upcoming items present.
steps: Open the sort order dropdown in the Timeline block
Select a different sort order option
Verify the Timeline items are reordered according to the selected sort order
test_cases.expected_result: Timeline items reorder to reflect the chosen sort order.

test_cases.title: Changing sort order preserves activities from all enrolled courses in Timeline
test_cases.workflow: Change sort order using sort order dropdown to update Timeline
test_cases.preconditions: User is on Dashboard with Timeline block visible and multiple upcoming items from different courses present.
steps: Open the sort order dropdown in the Timeline block
Select a different sort order option
Verify Timeline still lists upcoming activities and deadlines from multiple enrolled courses
test_cases.expected_result: Timeline remains populated with upcoming activities and deadlines across the user's enrolled courses and is ordered per the selected sort.

test_cases.title: Search activities by name returns matching Timeline items
test_cases.workflow: Search activities by name or type using search field
test_cases.preconditions: User is on Dashboard with the Timeline block visible and containing activities.
steps: Fill the search field with an activity name query
Click the Search button or press Enter
test_cases.expected_result: Timeline displays activities whose name matches the query.

test_cases.title: Search activities by type returns matching Timeline items
test_cases.workflow: Search activities by name or type using search field
test_cases.preconditions: User is on Dashboard with the Timeline block visible and containing activities of various types.
steps: Fill the search field with an activity type value
Click the Search button or press Enter
test_cases.expected_result: Timeline displays activities that match the specified type.

test_cases.title: Filter calendar events by a specific course
test_cases.workflow: Filter calendar events by course using "All courses" dropdown
test_cases.preconditions: Calendar block is visible in monthly view
steps: Select a specific course from the "All courses" dropdown
test_cases.expected_result: Calendar updates to show only events belonging to the selected course; the calendar heading shows the current month and year; dates with events display their names inline; the current date is highlighted.

test_cases.title: Show events from all courses using the All courses option
test_cases.workflow: Filter calendar events by course using "All courses" dropdown
test_cases.preconditions: Calendar block is visible in monthly view
steps: Select the "All courses" option from the "All courses" dropdown
test_cases.expected_result: Calendar displays events from all courses; the calendar heading shows the current month and year; dates with events display their names inline; the current date is highlighted.

test_cases.title: Create a personal calendar entry via New event
test_cases.workflow: Create personal calendar entries using "New event" button
test_cases.preconditions: User is signed in and Dashboard is visible.
steps: Click the Calendar block's "New event" button
Fill all required fields in the event form (event title and date, plus optional time if applicable)
Click the event form's Save/Submit button
test_cases.expected_result: A personal calendar entry is created and appears on its date in the Calendar block's monthly view.

test_cases.title: Dates with events display their names inline in monthly view
test_cases.workflow: Create personal calendar entries using "New event" button
test_cases.preconditions: A personal calendar event exists in the current month.
steps: Ensure the Calendar block shows the monthly view for the current month
Locate the date cell for the existing event and observe its contents
test_cases.expected_result: The event's name is displayed inline on the corresponding date in the monthly calendar view.

test_cases.title: Calendar shows monthly view with current month and year heading
test_cases.workflow: Create personal calendar entries using "New event" button
test_cases.preconditions: Dashboard is visible.
steps: Observe the Calendar block header
test_cases.expected_result: Calendar displays a monthly view with the current month and year as the heading.

test_cases.title: Calendar displays current month and highlights current date
test_cases.workflow: Navigate between months using left/right arrows
test_cases.preconditions: Dashboard page is open and the Calendar block is visible
steps: Observe the Calendar block heading
Observe the highlighted date for today in the calendar grid
Observe date cells that contain events and their inline event names
test_cases.expected_result: Calendar shows the current month and year in the heading, the current date is highlighted, and dates with events display their names inline.

test_cases.title: Navigate to next month using right arrow
test_cases.workflow: Navigate between months using left/right arrows
test_cases.preconditions: Dashboard page is open and the Calendar block is visible
steps: Click the Calendar block right arrow
Verify the month and year heading advances by one month
Verify the calendar grid updates and dates with events for the displayed month show their names inline
test_cases.expected_result: Calendar updates to the next month/year and displays the appropriate dates and inline event names for that month.

test_cases.title: Navigate to previous month using left arrow
test_cases.workflow: Navigate between months using left/right arrows
test_cases.preconditions: Dashboard page is open and the Calendar block is visible
steps: Click the Calendar block left arrow
Verify the month and year heading moves back by one month
Verify the calendar grid updates and dates with events for the displayed month show their names inline
test_cases.expected_result: Calendar updates to the previous month/year and displays the appropriate dates and inline event names for that month.

test_cases.title: Toggle Edit mode on shows Reset page to default button
test_cases.preconditions: None
steps: Click the Edit mode toggle to turn editing on
test_cases.expected_result: A "Reset page to default" button appears at the top right of the page.

test_cases.title: Toggle Edit mode on shows + Add a block and opens block types listing
test_cases.preconditions: None
steps: Click the Edit mode toggle to turn editing on
Click the "+ Add a block" button below the Dashboard heading
test_cases.expected_result: A page or panel listing all available block types is displayed.

test_cases.title: Add a block via + Add a block listing
test_cases.preconditions: Dashboard is in Edit mode
steps: Click the "+ Add a block" button
Select a block type from the listing and confirm adding the block
test_cases.expected_result: The selected block is added to the Dashboard and appears on the page.

test_cases.title: In Edit mode each block shows move icon and three-dot menu
test_cases.preconditions: Dashboard contains at least one existing block
steps: Click the Edit mode toggle to turn editing on
test_cases.expected_result: Each existing block shows a move icon and a three-dot menu for block actions.

test_cases.title: Configure an existing block via three-dot menu
test_cases.preconditions: Dashboard contains at least one existing block and is in Edit mode
steps: For an existing block, click its three-dot menu
Click the "Configure" action in the menu
test_cases.expected_result: The block configuration interface (dialog or settings panel) opens for that block.

test_cases.title: Delete an existing block via three-dot menu
test_cases.preconditions: Dashboard contains at least one existing block and is in Edit mode
steps: For an existing block, click its three-dot menu
Click the "Delete" action and confirm the deletion if prompted
test_cases.expected_result: The block is removed from the Dashboard and no longer appears on the page.

test_cases.title: Move an existing block using the move icon
test_cases.preconditions: Dashboard contains at least two blocks and is in Edit mode
steps: Click and drag the move icon of a block to a different position on the page
Release to place the block in the new position
test_cases.expected_result: The block's position on the Dashboard updates to the new location.

test_cases.title: Timeline does not update after changing sort order (regression negative)
test_cases.workflow: Change sort order using sort order dropdown to update Timeline
test_cases.preconditions: User is on Dashboard with Timeline block visible and multiple upcoming items present.
steps: Open the sort order dropdown in the Timeline block
Select a different sort order option
Observe whether the Timeline order changes
test_cases.expected_result: Timeline order fails to update and items remain in the previous order.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Timeline shows upcoming activities and deadlines across all enrolled courses

test_cases.title: Submit with all required fields empty
test_cases.workflow: Search activities by name or type using search field
test_cases.preconditions: User is on Dashboard with the Timeline block visible.
steps: Leave the search field empty
Click the Search button
test_cases.expected_result: Validation errors shown for all required fields.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Search field finds activities by name or type

test_cases.title: Per-block edit controls are not visible when Edit mode is off
test_cases.preconditions: Edit mode is off
steps: Observe an existing block on the Dashboard
test_cases.expected_result: The block does not show a move icon or a three-dot menu for configure, move, or delete actions.
test_cases.spec_evidence: each existing block shows a move icon

test_cases.title: Selecting a time range with no activities shows the empty state
test_cases.workflow: Select time range using time range dropdown to update Timeline
test_cases.preconditions: No activities exist within the selected time range across all enrolled courses
steps: Select a time range from the time range dropdown that contains no upcoming activities
Observe the Timeline block
test_cases.expected_result: The Timeline block displays its empty state indicating no activities in the selected range.
test_cases.spec_evidence: When no items exist within the selected range, an empty state is shown

test_cases.title: Search with non-matching query shows no results in Timeline
test_cases.workflow: Search activities by name or type using search field
test_cases.preconditions: User is on Dashboard with the Timeline block visible and populated with activities.
steps: Fill the search field with a query that does not match any activity name or type
Click the Search button or press Enter
test_cases.expected_result: Timeline shows an empty results state indicating no activities found.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Timeline shows upcoming activities and deadlines across all enrolled courses

test_cases.title: Filter to a course that has no events
test_cases.workflow: Filter calendar events by course using "All courses" dropdown
test_cases.preconditions: A course with no calendar events exists
steps: Select the course that has no events from the "All courses" dropdown
test_cases.expected_result: Month view shows no event names for any dates (no events displayed); the calendar heading shows the current month and year; the current date remains highlighted.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Dates with events display their names inline

test_cases.title: Multiple personal events on the same date display their names inline
test_cases.workflow: Create personal calendar entries using "New event" button
test_cases.preconditions: User is signed in and Dashboard is visible.
steps: Click the Calendar block's "New event" button; fill all required fields to create an event on a chosen date; click the event form's Save/Submit button
Repeat to create a second personal event on the same chosen date
Observe the date cell for that date in the monthly view
test_cases.expected_result: Both event names are displayed inline on that date in the monthly calendar view.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Dates with events display their names inline

test_cases.title: Navigate across year boundary using right arrow
test_cases.workflow: Navigate between months using left/right arrows
test_cases.preconditions: Dashboard page is open and the Calendar block is visible
steps: If the month/year heading shows December, click the Calendar block right arrow once; otherwise click the Calendar block right arrow repeatedly until the heading shows January of the following year
Verify the month and year heading reflects the year transition
Verify the calendar grid updates and dates with events for the displayed month show their names inline
test_cases.expected_result: Month/year heading correctly transitions across the year boundary (December→January) and the calendar displays the dates and inline event names for the new month.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Left/right arrows navigate between months

test_cases.title: Current date is not highlighted when viewing a different month
test_cases.workflow: Navigate between months using left/right arrows
test_cases.preconditions: Dashboard page is open and the Calendar block is visible
steps: Click the Calendar block left or right arrow to change the displayed month to one that is not the current month
Observe whether the calendar highlights the current date
Observe that dates with events for the displayed month show their names inline
test_cases.expected_result: When a month other than the current month is displayed, the current date is not highlighted and the calendar shows inline event names for the displayed month.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Current date is highlighted

test_cases.title: Change sort order when Timeline has a single upcoming item
test_cases.workflow: Change sort order using sort order dropdown to update Timeline
test_cases.preconditions: User is on Dashboard with Timeline block visible and only one upcoming item present.
steps: Open the sort order dropdown in the Timeline block
Select any sort order option
test_cases.expected_result: Timeline remains unchanged with the single upcoming item still displayed.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Timeline shows upcoming activities and deadlines across all enrolled courses

test_cases.title: Star a course via the course card's three-dot menu pins it to the top
test_cases.preconditions: At least two courses are visible in the My Courses listing
steps: Open the three-dot menu on a non-starred course card
Click "Star this course"
test_cases.expected_result: The selected course is pinned and appears at the top of the My Courses list.

test_cases.title: Filtering by Status = Starred shows pinned courses at the top
test_cases.preconditions: At least one course is already starred
steps: Open the status dropdown above the course grid and select "Starred"
test_cases.expected_result: The list is filtered to starred courses and the pinned course appears at the top of the filtered results.

test_cases.title: Remove a course from view via course card menu and verify it appears under Hidden
test_cases.preconditions: My Courses page is open and the target course card is visible
steps: Open the course card's three-dot menu for the target course and click "Remove from view"
Set the status dropdown to "Hidden"
Verify the target course appears in the grid and that its course card shows a banner image, the course name as a clickable link, and the category name
test_cases.expected_result: The course is hidden from the default listing and appears under the Hidden status with the expected course card elements.

test_cases.title: Course card displays banner image, clickable course name, and category name
test_cases.preconditions: At least one course card is visible in the course grid
steps: Inspect a course card in the course grid
test_cases.expected_result: The course card shows the course banner image, the course name as a clickable link, and the category name.

test_cases.title: My Courses shows status dropdown, search field, sort dropdown, and layout dropdown above grid
test_cases.preconditions: The My Courses listing is visible
steps: Inspect the controls area above the course grid
test_cases.expected_result: Four controls are present above the grid: a status dropdown, a search field, a sort dropdown, and a layout dropdown.

test_cases.title: Course card displays banner image, clickable course name, and category name
test_cases.preconditions: My Courses page is open and at least one course card is visible
steps: Inspect a course card in the grid
Verify the card shows a course banner image, the course name rendered as a clickable link, and the category name
test_cases.expected_result: Each course card displays the banner image, the course name as a clickable link, and the category name.

test_cases.title: My Courses shows status dropdown, search field, sort dropdown, and layout dropdown above the grid
test_cases.preconditions: My Courses page is open
steps: Locate the controls above the course grid
Verify presence of the status dropdown, the search field, the sort dropdown, and the layout dropdown
test_cases.expected_result: All four controls (status dropdown, search field, sort dropdown, layout dropdown) are present above the course grid.

test_cases.title: Removed course is not visible in non-Hidden status listings
test_cases.preconditions: My Courses page is open and the target course card is visible
steps: Open the course card's three-dot menu for the target course and click "Remove from view"
Set the status dropdown to a non-Hidden option (e.g., "All" or "In progress")
Verify the target course is not visible in the grid for the selected non-Hidden status
test_cases.expected_result: The removed course does not appear in non-Hidden status listings.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Remove from view hides the course without unenrolling

test_cases.title: Starring a second course pins the newly starred course above the previously starred course
test_cases.preconditions: At least two courses exist and one course is already starred
steps: Open the three-dot menu on a different non-starred course card
Click "Star this course"
test_cases.expected_result: The newly starred course is pinned to the top of the My Courses list, appearing above the previously starred course.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Star this course pins the course to the top

test_cases.title: Status dropdown contains the expected options
test_cases.preconditions: The My Courses listing is visible
steps: Open the status dropdown and inspect the list of available options
test_cases.expected_result: The dropdown includes the options: All, In progress, Future, Past, Starred, and Hidden.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Status dropdown options: All, In progress, Future, Past, Starred, Hidden

test_cases.title: Collapse all collapses every section at once
test_cases.workflow: Collapse all sections using the "Collapse all" link
test_cases.preconditions: Course page with multiple sections expanded and their contents visible
steps: Click the "Collapse all" link at the top right of the Course page
Verify every section's content area (activities and resources) is hidden
test_cases.expected_result: All sections are collapsed and their contents are not visible.

test_cases.title: Collapsed sections hide activities and resources including type icons and names
test_cases.workflow: Collapse all sections using the "Collapse all" link
test_cases.preconditions: Course page with sections containing activities and resources
steps: Click the "Collapse all" link at the top right
For each section, verify activity/resource rows are not visible and that type icons and clickable names are not displayed or interactable
test_cases.expected_result: Activities and resources (including their type icons and clickable names) are hidden and not interactable after collapsing.

test_cases.title: Toggle a section collapsed then expanded using its chevron
test_cases.workflow: Collapse or expand a section using the collapsible chevron
test_cases.preconditions: Course page is open and the section contains at least one activity or resource.
steps: Click the section's collapsible chevron to collapse the section
Verify the section's activities and resources are hidden
Click the same section's collapsible chevron to expand the section
Verify the section's activities and resources are visible with type icons and clickable names
test_cases.expected_result: Section collapses to hide its activities and re-expands to show activities with type icons and clickable names.

test_cases.title: Collapse one section without affecting other sections' expanded state
test_cases.workflow: Collapse or expand a section using the collapsible chevron
test_cases.preconditions: Course page is open with at least two sections visible and both expanded.
steps: Click the collapsible chevron for one section
Verify the clicked section's activities are hidden
Verify other section(s) remain in their previous visible/expanded state
test_cases.expected_result: Only the targeted section is collapsed; other sections retain their prior state.

test_cases.title: Student cannot enable Edit mode on course pages
test_cases.workflow: Collapse all sections using the "Collapse all" link
test_cases.preconditions: Logged in as a student on a Course page
steps: Attempt to enable Edit mode by clicking any Edit/Turn editing on control if present on the page
Observe whether Edit mode becomes enabled or the control/action is blocked
test_cases.expected_result: Edit mode is not enabled for the student (control absent or action not permitted).
test_cases.spec_evidence: Students cannot enable Edit mode on course pages

test_cases.title: Students cannot enable Edit mode on course pages
test_cases.workflow: Collapse or expand a section using the collapsible chevron
test_cases.preconditions: Course page is open as a student user.
steps: Attempt to click an 'Enable edit mode' control if it is present
Verify that edit mode is not enabled (control absent, disabled, or clicking has no effect)
test_cases.expected_result: Student user cannot enable Edit mode; the page remains in view-only mode.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Students cannot enable Edit mode on course pages

test_cases.title: Collapse all sets every section's collapsible chevron to collapsed state
test_cases.workflow: Collapse all sections using the "Collapse all" link
test_cases.preconditions: Course page with multiple expanded sections that show a collapsible chevron per section
steps: Click the "Collapse all" link at the top right
Verify each section's collapsible chevron updates to indicate the collapsed state
test_cases.expected_result: All section chevrons show the collapsed indicator after using "Collapse all".

test_cases.title: Collapse all preserves the currently highlighted active item in the Course Index
test_cases.workflow: Collapse all sections using the "Collapse all" link
test_cases.preconditions: Course page with at least one section expanded and a currently active item highlighted in the Course Index sidebar
steps: Note the currently highlighted item in the Course Index sidebar
Click the "Collapse all" link at the top right
Verify the same item in the Course Index remains highlighted
test_cases.expected_result: The previously highlighted active item remains highlighted after collapsing all sections.

test_cases.title: Each section displays a collapsible chevron and section name in the main content area
test_cases.workflow: Collapse or expand a section using the collapsible chevron
test_cases.preconditions: Course page is open with course sections listed.
steps: Observe the main content area
Verify each section shows a collapsible chevron adjacent to its section name
test_cases.expected_result: The main content area lists sections, each with a collapsible chevron and section name.

test_cases.title: Expanded section lists activities and resources with type icon and clickable name
test_cases.workflow: Collapse or expand a section using the collapsible chevron
test_cases.preconditions: Course page is open and the target section is expanded.
steps: If the section is collapsed, click its collapsible chevron to expand
Verify each activity/resource in the section shows a type icon and a clickable name
test_cases.expected_result: Within the expanded section, activities and resources are listed with a type icon and a clickable name.

test_cases.title: Clicking Collapse all when all sections are already collapsed maintains collapsed state without errors
test_cases.workflow: Collapse all sections using the "Collapse all" link
test_cases.preconditions: Course page where all collapsible sections are already collapsed
steps: Click the "Collapse all" link at the top right
Verify all sections remain collapsed and no error or unexpected UI change occurs
test_cases.expected_result: Sections remain collapsed and no error is shown after clicking "Collapse all."
test_cases.spec_evidence: A "Collapse all" link at the top right collapses all sections at once

test_cases.title: Apply a single filter condition and verify filtered results
test_cases.preconditions: Participants page is open with enrolled users present
steps: Use the Select attribute dropdown to add a single filter condition (choose an attribute and set a value that matches at least one participant)
Click "Apply filters"
test_cases.expected_result: Participants list updates to show only participants matching the condition and the table columns remain visible.

test_cases.title: Apply multiple filter conditions using + Add condition and verify combined filtering
test_cases.preconditions: Participants page is open with enrolled users present
steps: Use the Select attribute dropdown to add a first filter condition and click "+ Add condition" to add a second condition (set values that together match at least one participant)
Click "Apply filters"
test_cases.expected_result: Participants list updates to show only participants matching the combined conditions.

test_cases.title: Clear filters removes a single active condition and restores full participants list
test_cases.preconditions: Participants page has an active filter applied
steps: Ensure a single filter condition is active (via any toggle, attribute dropdown, or condition)
Click "Clear filters"
test_cases.expected_result: All filter conditions are removed and the participants table shows all enrolled users; table displays expected columns.

test_cases.title: Clear filters removes multiple active conditions and restores full participants list
test_cases.preconditions: Participants page has multiple active filter conditions
steps: Ensure multiple filter conditions are active (using toggles, attribute dropdown and added conditions)
Click "Clear filters"
test_cases.expected_result: All filter conditions are removed and the participants table shows all enrolled users; table displays expected columns.

test_cases.title: Participants table displays expected columns after clearing filters
test_cases.preconditions: Participants page has active filters
steps: Click "Clear filters"
Verify the participants table contains columns for checkbox, First/Last name, Roles, Groups, and Last access to course
test_cases.expected_result: Participants table shows the listed columns and rows reflect the unfiltered set of enrolled users.

test_cases.title: Filter participants by a chosen first-name initial
test_cases.preconditions: Participants page is open and contains participants with varied first-name initials.
steps: Click the First name alphabetical filter button for a chosen letter
test_cases.expected_result: Participants list shows only users whose first name starts with the chosen letter.

test_cases.title: Show full participants list using the First name 'All' filter
test_cases.preconditions: Participants page is open and multiple participants are enrolled.
steps: Click the First name 'All' alphabetical filter button
test_cases.expected_result: Participants table displays all enrolled users without initial-based filtering.

test_cases.title: Clicking a participant name opens their profile
test_cases.preconditions: Participants page is open and at least one participant is listed.
steps: Click a participant's First or Last name link in the participants table
test_cases.expected_result: The selected participant's profile page opens.

test_cases.title: Filter participants by selected Last name initial
test_cases.preconditions: Participants page is open and at least one participant has a last name starting with a visible letter
steps: Click a letter button (A–Z) in the Last name alphabetical filter
Observe the participants list
test_cases.expected_result: Participants list is filtered to show only users whose last name begins with the selected letter

test_cases.title: Selecting 'All' in Last name filter displays all enrolled participants
test_cases.preconditions: Participants page is open
steps: Click the 'All' button in the Last name alphabetical filter
Observe the participants list
test_cases.expected_result: Participants list shows all users enrolled in the course

test_cases.title: Clicking a participant name opens their profile from the Participants list
test_cases.preconditions: Participants page is open and at least one participant row is visible
steps: Click any participant's First or Last name link in the participants table
Observe the resulting view
test_cases.expected_result: The participant's profile is opened (profile view is displayed)

test_cases.title: Toggle sort by First name (ascending then descending)
test_cases.preconditions: Participants page is open and participants listing is visible
steps: Click the "First name" column header to sort the participants by first name
Verify the participants list is ordered by first name in ascending order
Click the "First name" column header again to reverse the sort
Verify the participants list is ordered by first name in descending order
test_cases.expected_result: Participants are sorted by first name in ascending order after first click and in descending order after the second click.

test_cases.title: Toggle sort by Last name (ascending then descending)
test_cases.preconditions: Participants page is open and participants listing is visible
steps: Click the "Last name" column header to sort the participants by last name
Verify the participants list is ordered by last name in ascending order
Click the "Last name" column header again to reverse the sort
Verify the participants list is ordered by last name in descending order
test_cases.expected_result: Participants are sorted by last name in ascending order after first click and in descending order after the second click.

test_cases.title: Open a participant's profile by clicking their name link
test_cases.preconditions: Participants page is open and participants listing is visible
steps: Click any participant's First/Last name link in the participants table
Observe that the participant's profile page opens
test_cases.expected_result: Clicking a participant name opens that participant's profile.

test_cases.title: Participants table displays required columns
test_cases.preconditions: Participants page is open and participants listing is visible
steps: Inspect the participants table header
test_cases.expected_result: Table contains columns for checkbox, First/Last name, Roles, Groups, and Last access to course.

test_cases.title: Participants table displays expected columns
test_cases.preconditions: Participants page is open with enrolled users present
steps: Ensure the participants table is visible on the page
Verify the table shows columns for checkbox, First/Last name, Roles, Groups, and Last access to course
test_cases.expected_result: Participants table displays the listed columns.

test_cases.title: First name alphabetical filter group contains All and A–Z buttons
test_cases.preconditions: Participants page is open.
steps: Verify the First name alphabetical filter group displays 'All' and buttons for letters A–Z
test_cases.expected_result: The First name filter shows the 'All' option and buttons for letters A–Z.

test_cases.title: Participants table displays expected columns
test_cases.preconditions: Participants page is open.
steps: Verify the participants table contains columns for checkbox, First/Last name, Roles, Groups, and Last access to course
test_cases.expected_result: Participants table shows the checkbox, First/Last name, Roles, Groups, and Last access to course columns.

test_cases.title: Last name alphabetical filter contains All and A–Z buttons
test_cases.preconditions: Participants page is open
steps: Locate the Last name alphabetical filter controls on the Participants page
Verify there is an 'All' button and buttons for letters A through Z in the Last name filter area
test_cases.expected_result: The Last name alphabetical filter shows 'All' and buttons for A–Z

test_cases.title: Participants table displays expected columns including Last access to course
test_cases.preconditions: Participants page is open
steps: Inspect the participants table header
Verify the table contains columns for checkbox, First/Last name, Roles, Groups, and Last access to course
test_cases.expected_result: Participants table shows the checkbox, First/Last name, Roles, Groups, and Last access to course columns

test_cases.title: Apply filters with all filter inputs empty
test_cases.preconditions: Participants page is open
steps: Leave all filter controls empty
Click "Apply filters"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation errors shown for all required filter fields or no filters are applied and the full participants list remains visible.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Participants page lists all users enrolled in the course

test_cases.title: First name filter applies to given names, not family names
test_cases.preconditions: Participants page is open and there exists a participant whose last name starts with the chosen letter but whose first name does not.
steps: Click the First name alphabetical filter button for the chosen letter
test_cases.expected_result: Participants whose last name but not first name start with the letter are not shown; filtering targets first names only.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Alphabetical filter buttons for First name and Last name include All and A–Z

test_cases.title: Sorted names remain clickable and open profile after sorting
test_cases.preconditions: Participants page is open and participants listing is visible
steps: Click the "First name" column header to sort the participants
Click any participant's First/Last name link from the sorted list
Observe that the participant's profile page opens
test_cases.expected_result: After sorting, participant names remain links and clicking a name opens the participant's profile.
test_cases.spec_evidence: First/Last name links to profile

test_cases.title: Apply filters that match no participants
test_cases.preconditions: Participants page is open with enrolled users present
steps: Use the Select attribute dropdown to add a filter condition with a value that matches no enrolled user
Click "Apply filters"
test_cases.expected_result: Participants table shows no rows and an appropriate 'no results' or empty-state message is displayed.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Participants page lists all users enrolled in the course

test_cases.title: Click Clear filters when no filters are active
test_cases.preconditions: Participants page with no active filters
steps: Ensure no filter conditions are active
Click "Clear filters"
test_cases.expected_result: No error occurs; participants list continues to show all enrolled users and table state remains unchanged.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Participants page lists all users enrolled in the course

test_cases.title: Clear filters when course has no enrolled users
test_cases.preconditions: Course has no enrolled users
steps: Ensure any active filters are cleared or click "Clear filters"
Observe the participants table
test_cases.expected_result: Participants table remains empty (no rows) and table columns remain visible; no errors are shown.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Participants page lists all users enrolled in the course

test_cases.title: Filter by a letter with no matching first names shows no results
test_cases.preconditions: Participants page is open and no participant has a first name starting with the chosen letter.
steps: Click the First name alphabetical filter button for a letter that no participant's first name starts with
test_cases.expected_result: Participants list shows no rows or a clear 'no participants' message for the selected initial.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Alphabetical filter buttons for First name and Last name include All and A–Z

test_cases.title: Display student's grades via the User report including course header and AGGREGATION row
test_cases.workflow: Display the student's own grades for the course via a User report
test_cases.preconditions: User report for the course is displayed for a student
steps: Confirm course name appears as a collapsible header in the User report
Click the course header to expand it
Observe that graded activities are listed indented beneath the header with columns Grade, Range, Percentage, Feedback, Contribution, and Calculated weight
Scroll to the bottom of the report and confirm an AGGREGATION Course total row is present
test_cases.expected_result: The User report shows the course header, indented graded activities with the listed columns, and an AGGREGATION Course total row.

test_cases.title: Verify graded activity shows earned grade and percentage
test_cases.workflow: Display the student's own grades for the course via a User report
test_cases.preconditions: User report contains at least one graded activity
steps: Locate a graded activity row in the User report
Verify the Grade column shows an earned numeric value and the Percentage column shows a corresponding percentage
test_cases.expected_result: Graded activity displays an earned numeric grade and the corresponding percentage.

test_cases.title: User report identifies the current student and displays only their grades
test_cases.workflow: Display the student's own grades for the course via a User report
test_cases.preconditions: User report for the course is displayed for a student
steps: Locate the user identification area or header in the User report
Verify the report corresponds to the current student and that grade rows correspond to that single user only
test_cases.expected_result: The User report is labeled for the current student and shows only that student's grades.

test_cases.title: Prevent student from viewing other students' grades or the full gradebook
test_cases.workflow: Display the student's own grades for the course via a User report
test_cases.preconditions: User report for the course is displayed for a student
steps: Attempt to switch the report view to a full gradebook or select a different user from any available user selector on the page
Observe whether other students' grades or a full gradebook view are presented
test_cases.expected_result: The UI prevents access to other students' grades or the full gradebook (no other students' grades are shown and access is denied or unavailable).
test_cases.spec_evidence: Students cannot access the full gradebook or other students' grades

test_cases.title: Collapse and expand course header hides and reveals graded activities
test_cases.workflow: Display the student's own grades for the course via a User report
test_cases.preconditions: User report for the course is displayed with at least one activity listed
steps: Click the course header to collapse it
Verify that graded activities are hidden from the list
Click the course header to expand it
Verify that graded activities are visible again and the currently active item is highlighted
test_cases.expected_result: Collapsing hides activities; expanding reveals them and the active item is highlighted.

test_cases.title: Display contribution to course total and calculated weight per activity
test_cases.workflow: Display the student's own grades for the course via a User report
test_cases.preconditions: User report lists activities that have weight and contribution data
steps: Locate an activity row that includes weight/contribution information
Verify Calculated weight and Contribution to course total columns are present and show values for that activity
test_cases.expected_result: Each activity row shows Calculated weight and Contribution to course total values where applicable.

test_cases.title: Display dash for ungraded activities in Grade column
test_cases.workflow: Display the student's own grades for the course via a User report
test_cases.preconditions: User report contains at least one ungraded activity
steps: Locate an ungraded activity row in the User report
Verify the Grade column displays "–" for that activity
test_cases.expected_result: The Grade column shows "–" for ungraded activities.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Grade is earned value or "–" if not yet graded

test_cases.title: Open submission form via Add submission
test_cases.preconditions: Assignment page is open and the assignment is accepting submissions
steps: Click "Add submission"
Observe the submission form area
test_cases.expected_result: Submission form opens and shows the configured input methods (online text editor and/or file upload area).

test_cases.title: Add submission using online text editor
test_cases.preconditions: Submission form includes an online text editor
steps: Click "Add submission"
Fill the online text editor with valid submission content
Click the submission form's "Submit" button
test_cases.expected_result: Submission status updates to "Submitted for grading" and the submission summary table reflects the submission (Last modified updated).

test_cases.title: Add submission using file upload area
test_cases.preconditions: Submission form includes a file upload area
steps: Click "Add submission"
Upload a valid file using the file upload area
Click the submission form's "Submit" button
test_cases.expected_result: Submission status updates to "Submitted for grading" and the submission summary table reflects the submission (Last modified updated).

test_cases.title: Add submission using both online text editor and file upload
test_cases.preconditions: Submission form includes both an online text editor and a file upload area
steps: Click "Add submission"
Fill the online text editor and upload a valid file
Click the submission form's "Submit" button
test_cases.expected_result: Submission status updates to "Submitted for grading" and the submission summary table reflects both text and uploaded file in the submission details.

test_cases.title: Edit submission before due date when resubmission permitted
test_cases.preconditions: A submission exists, the due date has not passed, and the teacher permits resubmission
steps: Click the submission's "Edit" or "Edit submission" action
Modify the submission content or uploaded file as needed
Click the submission form's "Submit" button
test_cases.expected_result: Submission is updated and the summary table's Last modified value reflects the update.

test_cases.title: View submitted work and submission metadata
test_cases.preconditions: Student has submitted the assignment and is viewing the Assignment page.
steps: Verify the Opened date, Due date and full Description are displayed on the page
Verify the Submission status section shows a summary table with rows for Submission status, Grading status, Time remaining, Last modified, and Submission comments
Click "View submission" and verify the submitted content (files or text) is visible
test_cases.expected_result: Submission content and all submission metadata are visible in the summary table and the full description and dates are displayed.

test_cases.title: View graded assignment shows earned grade and written feedback
test_cases.preconditions: The assignment has been graded for the student and the student is viewing the Assignment page.
steps: Verify the Grading status row reflects a graded state
Verify the earned grade and written feedback are displayed on the page
test_cases.expected_result: Grading status indicates graded and the earned grade plus written feedback are visible on the Assignment page.

test_cases.title: Open edit submission when resubmission permitted and before due date
test_cases.preconditions: Student has submitted the assignment, the due date has not passed, and the teacher permits resubmission.
steps: Click the "Edit submission" button in the Submission status section
Verify the submission editor/form opens and existing submission content is editable
test_cases.expected_result: Submission editor opens and the student can modify their submission.

test_cases.title: Submission status displays 'Submitted for grading' after submission
test_cases.preconditions: Student has submitted the assignment and is viewing the Assignment page.
steps: Verify the Submission status row shows 'Submitted for grading' in the summary table
Verify Time remaining and Last modified values are present alongside the status
test_cases.expected_result: Submission status reads 'Submitted for grading' and Time remaining and Last modified metadata are displayed.

test_cases.title: Edit and submit an online text-only submission
test_cases.preconditions: Assignment configured with online text editor; student has an existing submission; due date not passed; teacher permits resubmission.
steps: Click "Edit submission"
Fill the online text editor with updated submission text
Click "Save changes" or "Submit"
test_cases.expected_result: Submission is saved; Submission status shows 'Submitted for grading' and Last modified is updated.

test_cases.title: Edit and submit a file-only submission
test_cases.preconditions: Assignment configured with file upload area only; student has an existing submission; due date not passed; teacher permits resubmission.
steps: Click "Edit submission"
Attach a new file in the file upload area and remove or replace existing files as needed
Click "Save changes" or "Submit"
test_cases.expected_result: File upload is saved; Submission status shows 'Submitted for grading' and Last modified is updated.

test_cases.title: Edit and submit when both online text and file upload are enabled (update both)
test_cases.preconditions: Assignment configured with both online text editor and file upload; student has an existing submission; due date not passed; teacher permits resubmission.
steps: Click "Edit submission"
Update the online text editor and attach or replace files in the file upload area
Click "Save changes" or "Submit"
test_cases.expected_result: Both text and file changes are saved; Submission status shows 'Submitted for grading' and Last modified is updated.

test_cases.title: Attempt to edit submission when due date passed or resubmission not permitted
test_cases.preconditions: Student has submitted the assignment and the due date has passed or the teacher does not permit resubmission.
steps: Click the "Edit submission" button on the Assignment page
Observe whether the edit action is prevented or an explanatory message is shown
test_cases.expected_result: Edit action is not allowed and an appropriate message indicating resubmission is not permitted is displayed.
test_cases.spec_evidence: due date has not passed and the teacher permits resubmission

test_cases.title: Attempt to edit submission after due date has passed
test_cases.preconditions: Assignment due date has passed.
steps: Click "Edit submission"
test_cases.expected_result: Edit controls are not available and a message indicates editing is not permitted after the due date.
test_cases.spec_evidence: due date has not passed and the teacher permits

test_cases.title: Attempt to edit submission when teacher does not permit resubmission
test_cases.preconditions: Teacher has disabled resubmission for the assignment and due date has not passed.
steps: Click "Edit submission"
test_cases.expected_result: Edit controls are not available and a message indicates resubmission is not permitted by the teacher.
test_cases.spec_evidence: teacher permits resubmission

test_cases.title: Submission status section displays expected summary rows
test_cases.preconditions: Assignment page is open
steps: Locate the Submission status section
Inspect the summary table rows
test_cases.expected_result: The Submission status section shows a summary table with rows for Submission status, Grading status, Time remaining, Last modified, and Submission comments.

test_cases.title: Initial state shows no submissions message
test_cases.preconditions: Assignment page is open and no submission has been made
steps: Locate the Submission status section
Read the Submission status text
test_cases.expected_result: Submission status displays "No submissions have been made yet".

test_cases.title: View assignment with no submission made
test_cases.preconditions: Student has not made a submission for the assignment and is viewing the Assignment page.
steps: Verify the Submission status section summary table is present
Verify the Submission status row displays 'No submissions have been made yet' and no submitted content is shown
test_cases.expected_result: The summary table is shown and Submission status reads 'No submissions have been made yet', with no submission content available.

test_cases.title: Breadcrumbs show full navigation path and each segment is clickable
test_cases.preconditions: Student is viewing the assignment activity page.
steps: Verify breadcrumbs at the top show the full navigation path from the course to the activity with each segment rendered as a link
Click a breadcrumb segment and verify it navigates to the corresponding page
test_cases.expected_result: Breadcrumb segments are links representing the full path and clicking a segment navigates to the corresponding page.

test_cases.title: Course Index sidebar shows hierarchical tree, highlights active item, and can be closed
test_cases.preconditions: Student is viewing a course page with the Course Index sidebar open.
steps: Verify the Course Index sidebar presents a hierarchical tree with expand/collapse arrows for sections
Click a section's expand arrow and verify activities become visible and the current activity is highlighted
Click the sidebar close button (X) and verify the sidebar is hidden
test_cases.expected_result: The Course Index expands/collapses sections, the active item is highlighted, and the close button hides the sidebar.

test_cases.title: Edit only the online text when both editor and file upload are present
test_cases.preconditions: Assignment configured with both online text editor and file upload; student has an existing submission; due date not passed; teacher permits resubmission.
steps: Click "Edit submission"
Modify the online text editor content without changing files
Click "Save changes" or "Submit"
test_cases.expected_result: Text changes are saved; existing uploaded files remain unchanged; Last modified is updated.

test_cases.title: Submission status section shows expected summary rows
test_cases.preconditions: Assignment page open with Submission status section visible.
steps: Locate the Submission status section and view the summary table
test_cases.expected_result: Summary table contains rows for Submission status, Grading status, Time remaining, Last modified, and Submission comments.

test_cases.title: Assignment page displays Opened date, Due date, and Description
test_cases.preconditions: Assignment page is open.
steps: Locate the assignment header and description area on the page
test_cases.expected_result: The Opened date, Due date, and full Description are displayed.

test_cases.title: Submit with all required fields empty
test_cases.preconditions: Submission form is open
steps: Leave the online text editor and file upload area empty
Click the submission form's "Submit" button
test_cases.expected_result: Validation errors shown for required submission inputs and the submission is not accepted.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Submission form may include an online text editor, a file upload area, or both

test_cases.title: Submit with all required fields empty
test_cases.preconditions: Assignment form includes at least an online text editor or a file upload area.
steps: Leave all required fields empty
Click "Save changes" or "Submit"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation errors shown for required submission inputs.
test_cases.spec_evidence: online text editor, a file upload area, or both

test_cases.title: View submission when not yet graded shows 'Not graded' and no feedback
test_cases.preconditions: Student has submitted the assignment but it has not been graded.
steps: Verify the Grading status row displays the not-graded state
Verify that earned grade and written feedback are not present on the page
test_cases.expected_result: Grading status shows the not-graded state and there is no earned grade or written feedback displayed.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Grading status may be 'Not graded' or 'Graded'

test_cases.title: Attempt to edit submission after due date when resubmission not permitted
test_cases.preconditions: A submission exists and the assignment due date has passed
steps: Attempt to click the submission's "Edit" or "Edit submission" action
Observe the result or any messages preventing editing
test_cases.expected_result: Editing is not allowed (no edit form opens or an explanatory message is shown).
test_cases.spec_evidence: if the due date has not passed

test_cases.title: Forums section is collapsed by default in Course Index sidebar
test_cases.workflow: Expand Forums section via an arrow
test_cases.preconditions: Course page is open and Course Index sidebar is visible
steps: Observe the Forums section in the Course Index sidebar
Verify the Forums section is in collapsed state and the expand arrow is visible
test_cases.expected_result: Forums section is collapsed by default and shows an expand arrow

test_cases.title: Expand Forums section via arrow reveals forum activities grouped under Forums
test_cases.workflow: Expand Forums section via an arrow
test_cases.preconditions: Forums section is visible and collapsed
steps: Click the Forums section expand arrow
Verify forum activities are displayed under the Forums header and appear grouped by type
test_cases.expected_result: Forums section expands and forum activities are shown grouped under Forums

test_cases.title: Expand Resources section via its arrow
test_cases.workflow: Expand Resources section via an arrow
test_cases.preconditions: Course Index sidebar is visible and Resources section is collapsed
steps: Click the Resources section's expand arrow
Observe the Resources section's content area
test_cases.expected_result: Resources section expands and its contained resource items become visible.

test_cases.title: Expanded Resources shows resource items grouped by type
test_cases.workflow: Expand Resources section via an arrow
test_cases.preconditions: Course Index sidebar is visible and Resources section is collapsed
steps: Click the Resources section's expand arrow
Inspect the list of items displayed under the expanded Resources section
test_cases.expected_result: Resource items are displayed and grouped by resource type under the Resources section.

test_cases.title: Expand an additional activity type collapsible section to reveal its activities
test_cases.workflow: Expand or collapse an additional activity type collapsible section
test_cases.preconditions: Course page is open and shows additional activity type collapsible sections in the Course Index sidebar
steps: Click the expand arrow for an additional activity type collapsible section
Observe the content area under that section
test_cases.expected_result: The section expands and the activities of that type are displayed grouped under the section.

test_cases.title: Collapse an expanded additional activity type collapsible section to hide its activities
test_cases.workflow: Expand or collapse an additional activity type collapsible section
test_cases.preconditions: Course page is open and the target additional activity type collapsible section is expanded
steps: Click the collapse arrow for the expanded additional activity type collapsible section
Observe the content area under that section
test_cases.expected_result: The section collapses and the activities of that type are hidden.

test_cases.title: Expanding a section reveals the currently active item and preserves its highlight
test_cases.workflow: Expand or collapse an additional activity type collapsible section
test_cases.preconditions: Course page is open and the currently active activity is located inside a collapsed additional activity type section
steps: Click the expand arrow for the additional activity type collapsible section that contains the active activity
Observe the list of activities within the expanded section
test_cases.expected_result: The active activity becomes visible and remains highlighted in the list after expansion.

test_cases.title: Resources section is collapsed by default
test_cases.workflow: Expand Resources section via an arrow
test_cases.preconditions: Course Index sidebar is visible
steps: Observe the Resources section without interacting with the page
test_cases.expected_result: Resources section appears collapsed and its child items are hidden by default.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Forums section and Resources section are collapsed by default

test_cases.title: Collapse Forums section via arrow hides forum activities
test_cases.workflow: Expand Forums section via an arrow
test_cases.preconditions: Forums section is expanded
steps: Click the Forums section expand arrow
Verify forum activities are hidden and the section returns to collapsed state
test_cases.expected_result: Forums section collapses and forum activities are hidden

test_cases.title: Expand multiple additional activity type sections independently
test_cases.workflow: Expand or collapse an additional activity type collapsible section
test_cases.preconditions: Course page is open with at least two additional activity type collapsible sections present
steps: Click the expand arrow for one additional activity type collapsible section
Click the expand arrow for a different additional activity type collapsible section
test_cases.expected_result: Both sections are expanded simultaneously and each displays the activities for its respective type.

test_cases.title: Expand arrow missing or non-responsive for Forums section
test_cases.workflow: Expand Forums section via an arrow
test_cases.preconditions: Forums section is visible
steps: Check for presence of the Forums section expand arrow
Click the Forums section expand arrow if present
test_cases.expected_result: Forum activities remain hidden after clicking the arrow (no expand behavior)
test_cases.spec_evidence: Forums section and Resources section are expandable via an arrow

test_cases.title: Clicking Resources header outside the arrow does not expand the section
test_cases.workflow: Expand Resources section via an arrow
test_cases.preconditions: Course Index sidebar is visible and Resources section is collapsed
steps: Click the Resources section header area excluding the expand arrow
Verify whether the Resources section expands or remains collapsed
test_cases.expected_result: Resources section remains collapsed; expansion occurs only via the arrow control.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Forums section and Resources section are expandable via an arrow

test_cases.title: Expanded Forums section displays a consolidated overview of forum activities grouped by type
test_cases.workflow: Expand Forums section via an arrow
test_cases.preconditions: Course page is open and the course contains multiple forum activities
steps: Click the Forums section expand arrow
Verify the expanded view lists all forum activities and they are grouped under the Forums heading
test_cases.expected_result: A consolidated overview of every activity in the course is displayed, grouped by type
test_cases.spec_evidence: Provides a consolidated overview of every activity in the course, grouped by type

test_cases.title: Repeatedly toggling an additional activity type section preserves the consolidated grouping
test_cases.workflow: Expand or collapse an additional activity type collapsible section
test_cases.preconditions: Course page is open and the target additional activity type collapsible section is present
steps: Click the expand arrow for the additional activity type collapsible section
Click the collapse arrow for the same section
Click the expand arrow for the same section again
test_cases.expected_result: The activities reappear and remain grouped by type as before; the consolidated overview is preserved after repeated toggles.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Provides a consolidated overview of every activity in the course, grouped by type

test_cases.title: Open Edit profile form shows collapsible panels and expected fields
test_cases.workflow: Clicking "Edit profile" opens the profile form
test_cases.preconditions: User is on their Profile page
steps: Click "Edit profile" link
Click each collapsible panel header (General, User picture, Additional names, Interests, Optional fields) to expand them
test_cases.expected_result: The profile form is displayed; each named panel expands to reveal its fields including First name, Last name, Email address, Email visibility dropdown, MoodleNet profile ID, City/town, Country dropdown, Timezone dropdown, Description, current user picture, new picture upload and picture description, Additional names, Interests, and the Update profile and Cancel controls.

test_cases.title: Profile header displays initials icon, full name, Message button, and optional description
test_cases.workflow: Clicking "Edit profile" opens the profile form
test_cases.preconditions: User is on their Profile page
steps: Locate the student's circular initials icon, full name, and "Message" button in the profile header
Observe the profile description area for presence or absence
test_cases.expected_result: Profile header shows the circular initials icon, full name, a "Message" button, and the profile description area which may be empty or contain text.

test_cases.title: Update profile with valid required fields
test_cases.workflow: Update profile validates all required fields before saving
test_cases.preconditions: Profile form is open
steps: Fill all required fields with valid data (First name, Last name, Email address, Country, Timezone, and any other required fields)
Click "Update profile"
test_cases.expected_result: Profile saved and updated values displayed on the profile page.

test_cases.title: Cancel discards edited profile field values
test_cases.preconditions: Profile form is open for the current user
steps: Fill editable profile fields (First name, Last name, City/town, Description, Interests) with new values
Click "Cancel"
test_cases.expected_result: Profile form closes and the profile displays the original values; none of the edits were saved.

test_cases.title: Cancel discards a newly selected user picture
test_cases.preconditions: Profile form is open for the current user and a current picture is displayed
steps: Change user picture by selecting a new picture file and enter a picture description
Click "Cancel"
test_cases.expected_result: Profile form closes and the displayed user picture remains the previous/current picture; the new upload is not saved.

test_cases.title: Prevent a student from editing another student's profile
test_cases.workflow: Clicking "Edit profile" opens the profile form
test_cases.preconditions: User is on another student's Profile page
steps: Attempt to click the "Edit profile" link on the other student's Profile page
test_cases.expected_result: The Edit profile form is not accessible; editing controls are not available and the user cannot modify the profile.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Students can only modify their own profile

test_cases.title: Attempt to update another user's profile is blocked
test_cases.workflow: Update profile validates all required fields before saving
test_cases.preconditions: Profile form for another user is open
steps: Modify one or more editable fields
Click "Update profile"
test_cases.expected_result: Changes are not saved and the system prevents the update (authorization error or redirect).
test_cases.spec_evidence: Students can only modify their own profile

test_cases.title: Edit control not available for another user's profile (students)
test_cases.preconditions: Viewing a profile belonging to another user while signed in as a student
steps: Inspect the profile page for the presence of an "Edit profile" control or button
test_cases.expected_result: The "Edit profile" control is not present or is disabled for the student viewing another user's profile.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Students can only modify their own profile

test_cases.title: Save profile with Description left empty (Description optional)
test_cases.workflow: Update profile validates all required fields before saving
test_cases.preconditions: Profile form is open
steps: Fill all required fields with valid data and leave the Description field empty
Click "Update profile"
test_cases.expected_result: Profile saved successfully and Description remains empty on the profile page.

test_cases.title: Replace user picture and save profile
test_cases.workflow: Update profile validates all required fields before saving
test_cases.preconditions: Profile form is open
steps: Upload a new user picture and fill all required fields
Click "Update profile"
test_cases.expected_result: New picture is saved and displayed as the current picture on the profile page.

test_cases.title: Cancel discards changes to optional profile fields only
test_cases.preconditions: Profile form is open for the current user
steps: Modify only optional fields (Additional names, Interests, Description)
Click "Cancel"
test_cases.expected_result: Profile form closes and optional fields remain unchanged on the profile; no optional-field edits are saved.

test_cases.title: Submit with all required fields empty
test_cases.workflow: Update profile validates all required fields before saving
test_cases.preconditions: Profile form is open
steps: Leave all required fields empty
Click "Update profile"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation errors shown for all required fields.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Update profile validates all required fields before saving

test_cases.title: Open Edit profile when Description is empty
test_cases.workflow: Clicking "Edit profile" opens the profile form
test_cases.preconditions: User is on their Profile page with an empty profile description
steps: Click "Edit profile" link
Open the General panel and inspect the Description field
test_cases.expected_result: The profile form opens normally and the Description field may be empty without error or blocking the form.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Profile description is optional

test_cases.title: Cancel with no changes closes form without affecting profile
test_cases.preconditions: Profile form is open for the current user
steps: Click "Cancel" without modifying any fields
Verify the profile display after the form closes
test_cases.expected_result: Form closes and the profile data remains unchanged.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Cancel exits without changes

test_cases.title: Log out terminates the current session and redirects to login page
test_cases.workflow: Selecting Log out terminates the current authenticated session and redirects to the login page.
test_cases.preconditions: User is authenticated and the user menu is visible
steps: Open the user menu via the user's initials icon
Click "Log out"
test_cases.expected_result: The current authenticated session is terminated and the application redirects to the login page.

test_cases.title: Access to protected pages requires re-authentication after logout
test_cases.workflow: Selecting Log out terminates the current authenticated session and redirects to the login page.
test_cases.preconditions: User has logged out and the login page is displayed
steps: From the current page, click a protected page link in the top navigation (for example: "Dashboard")
Observe whether the protected page is displayed or whether the login page is shown
test_cases.expected_result: Access to the protected page is blocked and the user remains on or is redirected to the login page; re-authentication is required to view the protected page.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Access to all protected pages requires re-authentication after logout
